Income tax provision; supply and other revenue; fixed assets; cloud computing; lease cash application; balance sheet and income statement misclassifications; and inappropriately recognized revenue in the ATI business primarily related to fiscal year 2025.

The Expense Classification error did not result in any change in total operating expenses (company-operated store expenses were overstated and supply and other expenses were equally understated in the corresponding periods).
The errors result in overstatements of cash and revenue and understatement of selling, general and administrative expense in the consolidated statement of operations for fiscal years 2023 and 2024.

Neither emerging-growth-company box is checked. The first reads: 'Indicate by check mark whether the registrant is an emerging growth company as defined in Rule 405 of the Securities Act of 1933 (§230.405 of this chapter) or Rule 12b-2 of the Securities Act of 1934 (§240.12b-2 of this chapter).' The second reads: 'If an emerging growth company, indicate by check mark if the registrant has elected not to use the extended transition period for complying with any new or revised financial accounting standards provided pursuant to Section 13(a) of the Exchange Act.'

No. The 8-K states that the Company's review of the financial statements and evaluation of its internal controls over financial reporting is ongoing and the Company may identify further material errors.

The previously issued consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2024 and fiscal year 2023 (as contained in the FY2024 Form 10-K), the previously issued unaudited condensed consolidated financial statements for each quarterly and year-to-date period within fiscal year 2024, and the quarterly and year-to-date periods ended September 27, 2025, June 28, 2025 and March 29, 2025 should not be relied upon. The impact of the lease adjustments, cash adjustments, expense classification, and other errors described will be reflected in the Company's Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year 2025.

Three classes of forward-looking statements: (i) statements regarding the Company's plans and expectations; (ii) statements regarding materiality or significance of the misstatements and adjustments presented (as management's best estimates as of the date hereof); (iii) statements regarding the restatement's quantitative effects, the effectiveness of the restated financial statements, and any anticipated conclusions of the Company, the Audit Committee, or the Board of Directors. Identified risk-factor categories include the risk that additional information may arise out of the continuing review of the financial statements and the evaluation of accounting practices, the preparation of the Company's restated financial statements or other subsequent events that would require the Company to make additional adjustments, as well as inherent limitations in internal control over financial reporting.
